mechanism_narrative: A persistent low or declining federal funds rate lowers the equity discount rate and cost of capital, mechanically raising the present value of future corporate earnings and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.69% indicates accommodative policy stance
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level/trend and 20-day forward SPX returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window, or returns following low-rate periods are not statistically distinguishable from high-rate periods (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions that precede earnings downgrades and consumption slowdown, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.9% near cycle lows, sensitive to inflection
failure_condition: In periods where the 0.3pp trigger fires, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC is below 0.03 across two consecutive out-of-sample years.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and industrial production (INDPRO) indicates synchronized demand and supply-side expansion, reinforcing confidence in the growth cycle and generating positive forward equity returns; neither series alone is sufficient because divergence between consumption and production signals imbalance rather than broad expansion.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 497559 and INDPRO at 107.1665 both in expansion range
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and INDPRO fails to outperform single-series RSAFS-only signal by Rank IC margin > 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, indicating no genuine interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is above a restrictive threshold while CPI inflation remains elevated, monetary tightening regime dominates equity pricing, causing short-horizon SPX returns to react negatively to further inflation surprises due to fear of additional hikes; this mechanism is regime-specific and inactive when FEDFUNDS is low.
evidence_refs: fact:current FEDFUNDS at 1.69% is below trigger threshold, so mechanism currently dormant
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with elevated inflation
failure_condition: When trigger condition is met, 5-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the effect disappears (Rank IC < 0.02) once tested against a placebo threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflect ongoing labor demand strength that supports consumer income and near-term corporate revenue, generating a modest positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the following week.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 148424 thousand persons, latest available print
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following positive PAYEMS prints are not significantly different from returns following negative prints (t-test p > 0.10) over a 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year decline in industrial production exceeding 1% signals an emerging manufacturing contraction that historically precedes broader earnings recessions, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter as markets price in slower corporate output growth.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 107.1665, near recent range, requires historical YoY comparison to trigger
failure_condition: Triggered INDPRO contraction episodes show positive 60-day forward SPX returns in over 55% of cases, or Rank IC falls below 0.03 in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) weaken disproportionately when the federal funds rate is simultaneously elevated, because higher financing costs compound with reduced housing demand to signal broader consumer retrenchment; this joint deterioration predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the following month, whereas either signal alone is insufficient given normal rate-housing correlation.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1319 thousand units and FEDFUNDS at 1.69% currently in low-rate regime
regime_hint: rising rate environment with softening housing
failure_condition: The joint HOUST-FEDFUNDS interaction term fails to improve Rank IC by more than 0.02 relative to HOUST alone over a 3-year rolling test, indicating no true interaction effect exists.

mechanism_narrative: In a disinflationary regime where CPI growth is decelerating toward target, equity markets price in future rate cuts and re-rate valuations upward, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter; in a re-accelerating inflation regime the same CPI deceleration signal loses predictive power or reverses.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 250.013 with FEDFUNDS at 1.69% indicative of low-rate disinflationary backdrop
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns show no significant difference (p > 0.10) between decelerating-CPI periods and accelerating-CPI periods, or the direction of the effect flips sign in more than one out of three tested regimes without being captured by the regime_hint.
